congress_website_title: AN ACT INSTITUTIONALIZING A NATIONAL GOVERNMENT ETHICAL HACKING PROGRAM AND CYBERSECURITY WORKFORCE DEVELOPMENT, AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR
congress_website_abstract: Institutionalizes a National Government Ethical Hacking Program aimed at identifying and resolving security vulnerabilities in government information systems before they can be exploited. Proposes that the program will be administered by the Department of Information and Communications Technology (DICT), in partnership with the Cybercrime Investigation and Coordinating Center (CICC) and the Department of National Defense (DND). Defines several terms, including Bug Bounty - a reward or incentive offered for the discovery and responsible disclosure of a security vulnerability in a government system.

congress_website_abstract: Seeks to propose several amendments to RA11861, some of which are as follows: SEC. 4. CATEGORIES OF SOLO PARENT, -...(a) A parent who provides sole parental care and support of the child or children due to - (4) Physical or mental incapacity of the spouse ... PROVIDED THAT THE INCAPACITY IS SUBSTANTIAL, ENOUGH TO PREVENT PROPER EXECUTION OF PARENTAL AUTHORITY. (e)...in cases of grandparent, WHO MAY NOT BE MARRIED, who are senior citizens but who have sole parental care and support over their grandchildren ... PROVIDED FURTHER, THAT PWDs WHO QUALIFY AS SOLO PARENTS SHALL LIKEWISE BE ENTITLED TO THE BENEFIT OF THIS ACT IN ADDITION TO THE BENEFITS GRANTED TO THEM BY RA07277 (Magna Carta for PWD) AND RA10754 (Expanding the Benefits and Privileges of PWD); SEC.4. Section 5 of RA No.11861 is amended to read as follows: “SEC. 5. COMPREHENSIVE PACKAGE OF SOCIAL PROTECTION SERVICES... (e) Targeted interventions for individuals in need of protection which include ... OSAEC AND CYBER SAFETY PROGRAMS, EMOTIONAL AND MENTAL HEALTH INTERVENTIONS, FINANCIAL LITERACY AND ENTREPRENEURIAL EDUCATION PROGRAMS, AND REPRODUCTIVE HEALTH EDUCATION.”

congress_website_abstract: Seeks to create a Head-Start Program to provide free essential supplies and medical support for newborns of indigent mothers. Proposes several components of the Program, among others, as follows: a) Free access to newborn screening; b) Provision of "Newborn Starter Kits" to all newborns of indigent mothers; c) Newborn care and nutrition seminars for parents; and d) Special support programs for indigent families, including supplemental nutrition and healthcare services.
